name: Differential ZIP Backup

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.10

15th July, 2026

I originally built parts of this rather quickly, and I am now fixing the rough edges while putting Fancy Kit through its paces. It should be getting a little better, one step at a time.

### Fixed

- `All and delete extra` now restores every selected file successfully before removing local files represented by the selected deletion records.
- Restore points now use the state recorded at the selected historical revision, including files deleted or recreated later.
- Missing archives, missing ZIP entries, unreadable archives, and Vault write failures are now reported, and planned deletions do not begin when restoration fails.
- Deletion failures are now reported instead of being treated as successful completion.
- The legacy folder restore now skips deletion records and restores the available file revisions.

### Improved

- A restore that will delete local files now uses the explicit `Restore and Delete Confirmation` title and `Restore and delete` action. `Cancel` remains the default.
- Restore planning and execution use separate screen wake-lock leases, so reviewing the confirmation does not keep the screen awake.
- The confirmation presents a point-in-time restore plan. If the Vault changes while it is open, cancel the operation and prepare the restore again.

### What to verify

- [x] Install `0.1.10` through BRAT from the GitHub pre-release and confirm that DiffZip loads with existing settings preserved.
- [x] Confirm that the installed plug-in version is `0.1.10`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.9

14th July, 2026

I had kept this feature on the back burner for a long time, but I have finally implemented it. I expect to add more small improvements like this over time. The [Fancy Kit](https://github.com/vrtmrz/fancy-kit) toolbox made the work much easier; have a look if it interests you.

### New features

- DiffZip now requests a best-effort screen wake lock during backup, restore, and selective-sync Fetch and Send operations.
- Overlapping and nested operations share one platform wake lock.

### Improved

- Restore completion now waits for every selected archive file to finish writing before completion is reported and the screen wake lock is released.

The prerelease was reviewed on a real mobile Obsidian installation. The final build is unchanged apart from its version number.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.9-wakelock.1

14th July, 2026

I had kept this feature on the back burner for a long time, but I have finally implemented it. I expect to add more small improvements like this over time. The [Fancy Kit](https://github.com/vrtmrz/fancy-kit) toolbox made the work much easier; have a look if it interests you.

### New features

- DiffZip now requests a best-effort screen wake lock during backup, restore, and selective-sync Fetch and Send operations.
- Overlapping and nested operations share one platform wake lock.

### Improved

- Restore completion now waits for every selected archive file to finish writing before completion is reported and the screen wake lock is released.

### What to verify

- [x] Install `0.1.9-wakelock.1` through BRAT and confirm existing settings are preserved.
- [x] On mobile, run a backup and confirm the display remains awake during the operation.
- [x] After the backup finishes, confirm normal screen timeout behaviour resumes.
- [x] Restore a file and confirm the display remains awake until the restored file has been written.
- [x] Confirm the restored content matches the selected revision.
- [x] Run selective-sync Fetch and Send and confirm the display remains awake during each operation.
- [x] Confirm cancellation and errors do not leave the display permanently awake.

I will record the results on [PR #17](https://github.com/vrtmrz/diffzip/pull/17).
releases.name: 0.1.9-wakelock.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.8

10th July, 2026

### Improved

- Refactored the restore confirmation to use the Fancy Kit UI interaction contract while preserving its existing behaviour.
- Added consumer-owned harness coverage and a local real-Obsidian scenario for rendered Markdown, explicit cancellation, and dismissal.

I have recorded the results on [PR #14](https://github.com/vrtmrz/diffzip/pull/14).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.8-fancy.1

10th July, 2026

I am carrying out a bit of refactoring to improve robustness and maintainability. This release is intended for a comprehensive review—including CI and testing with a real Obsidian instance—and does not offer any particular benefits. You are welcome to skip it.

I, vrtmrz, will review this release. Thank you as always!

### Changed

- Refactored the restore confirmation to use the Fancy Kit UI interaction contract.
- Added a consumer-owned harness while preserving the existing restore confirmation behaviour.

### What to verify

Use a disposable or backed-up Vault and record the Obsidian version, BRAT version, OS/device, and test date.

- [ ] Install `0.1.8-fancy.1` through BRAT, enable DiffZip, reload Obsidian, and confirm existing settings are preserved without new errors
- [ ] Open a real restore confirmation and confirm its Markdown summary and expected Vault-relative file path render correctly
- [ ] Click **Cancel** and confirm no file is restored or modified
- [ ] Dismiss with Escape/back and confirm no file is restored or modified
- [ ] Click **Yes, restore them!** and confirm only the selected file is restored with the selected revision content
- [ ] Confirm the dialog content and actions are usable on desktop
- [ ] Confirm the dialog content and actions are usable on mobile

I will record the results on [PR #13](https://github.com/vrtmrz/diffzip/pull/13).
releases.name: 0.1.8-fancy.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.7

7th July, 2026

This release refreshes the main backup, restore, and sync workflows, with clearer commands and a new restore revision selector.

### Highlights

- Added a new `Restore from Backup` revision selector UI.
- Simplified the command palette.
  - Main commands are now `Create Differential Backup`, `Restore from Backup`, and `Sync Remote Backup`.
  - Older direct backup/restore variants are now hidden behind `Show legacy commands`.

- Improved backup batching.
  - Backups can now split work by both file count and total source size.
  - TOC updates are committed per completed batch.
  - Failed TOC writes roll back just-created ZIP files where possible.

### Fixes and Improvements

- Fixed filtered restore-point selection so only files matching the current filter are selected.
- Improved restore progress and dialogue width handling on mobile.
- Clarified backup behaviour for standard, only-new, and non-destructive modes (doc).
- Clarified that `Auto backup style` applies only to `Start backup at launch` (doc).
- Improved handling and documentation for ZIP splitting and encrypted ZIP compatibility (doc).

### Testing and CI

- Added GitHub Actions CI for build, lint, Deno tests, and Svelte UI tests.
- Added Svelte/Vitest UI coverage for the restore revision selector.
- Added more backup pipeline, sync engine, archive, and compatibility tests.
- Added compatibility checks for common ZIP tools where available.

## 0.1.0

14th May, 2026

### New feature
- Lightweight `sync` has been implemented
  In other words, you can import and send updated files all from a single dialogue.
- Progress is now displayed in a dialogue box.

### Improved
- Got a lot quicker.

### Internal changes
- Many tests have been added.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.21 

13th May, 2026

## New features

### Selective Apply a file (Lightweight Synchronisation)
1. Perform `Selective Apply Remote Backup (Check and Mirror)` from the command palette.
2. Select the file you want to apply.
3. Perform `Apply` to apply the differences.

releases.description:
New feature:
- Restore File Selection dialogue has been implemented
- Now we can backup only newer files
- Now we can apply deletion
- We can restore the vault totally.
- And we can restore the vault only to the newer files.

Tidied
- Many files have been rewritten and abstracted.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.11
Fixed: 
- We can update things to R2 or some compatible object storage Using Custom HTTP Handler.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.10
## Fixed:
- We can restore files even on Windows again!

## Improved:
- S3 compatible buckets are now supported for backup destinations.
- Folder restoration implemented
- We can encrypt each archived backup-zip file in AES-CBC.
  - Note: Not compatible with the encrypted zip file. We have to decrypt the file by OpenSSL, without this plug-in. 
- We can split the backup file by a number of archived files, for efficiency on restore.

And it may some more improvements.
